name: Hot Lips Page
abstract: Oran Thaddeus "Hot Lips" Page (January 27, 1908 – November 5, 1954) was an American jazz trumpeter, singer, and bandleader. He was known as a scorching soloist and powerful vocalist.

about_quotes.speaker: Big Nick Nicholas
about_quotes.evidence: "Lips" liked me and he said he wanted me, you know, to record with him. We rehearsed and we did it for Columbia and he recorded one of my tunes called "La Danse"

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Waters
about_quotes.evidence: I worked with Lips Page several times... I worked at Small's Paradise with Hot Lips Page also and made arrangements for him.

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Waters
about_quotes.evidence: Starting in 1933, Benny played for ten years in the bands of Fletcher Henderson, Jimmie Lunceford and Hot Lips Page.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Waters interviewed by Michael Woods, Los Angeles, California, September 1, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Vic Dickenson
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions Lips Page; the interviewee does not respond with any personal anecdote or connection.

about_quotes.speaker: Freddie Moore
about_quotes.evidence: >> [FRED WELBURN] Let's see... If I'm not mistaken, I think Hot Lips Page was with that band.

about_quotes.speaker: Jones Hank
about_quotes.evidence: When I first arrived in New York, I worked with the Hot Lips Page Group.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jones Hank — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
